assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items reference escalating Iran-related tensions and Strait of Hormuz dynamics, but do not report any major U.S. passenger airline publicly announcing a ≥7-day suspension of service to a listed Gulf hub explicitly citing security risk or regional conflict.

No earnings release or SEC filing is cited in today’s bullets indicating Delta reduced/withdrew guidance or issued a material caution explicitly tied to higher fuel costs and/or Middle East conflict impacts.

While several stories discuss the Strait of Hormuz and oil/shipping conditions, there is no evidence here that Lloyd’s/JWC added a new 'Listed Areas' geographic entry covering the Strait of Hormuz/Persian Gulf approaches.

No referenced EASA Conflict Zone Information Bulletin (CZIB) or equivalent formal safety communication appears in today’s bullets advising avoidance/mitigations in Gulf state airspace due to heightened security risk.
Today’s coverage does not include a DoD announcement of a single new ≥$300M contract/OTA to a venture-backed defense-tech company focused on drones/counter-drone/ISR/AI targeting.
No China nationwide rule/administrative measure on mandatory Remote ID for civilian drones is described in today’s bullets.
There is no mention today of a SpaceX public U.S. regulatory filing (e.g., FCC) explicitly proposing an on-orbit computing/data-center technology demonstration with a named payload type or partner.
The Broadcom items mention expanded chip deals with Google and Anthropic, but do not indicate Anthropic announced a new funding round/strategic investment raising ≥$5B in disclosed new capital.

Today’s bullets do not include NSIDC (or comparable dataset) reporting about the 2026 Arctic sea-ice minimum ranking within the five lowest minima; this is also seasonally premature relative to the usual September minimum.
